question: How do you structure a Terraform repository for a data engineering team with multiple environments?
answer: Common pattern: a modules/ directory with reusable building blocks (vpc, rds, s3-data-lake, emr-cluster) and an environments/ directory with dev/, staging/, prod/ subdirectories each containing main.tf that calls modules with environment-specific vars. Use a shared remote state backend and separate state files per environment. Terragrunt can automate the backend config boilerplate. Keep modules versioned in Git tags so environments can pin to specific versions.
